text: Attention mechanisms usually achieve significant improvements in the feature representations by modeling the spatial or channel relationships. However, the channel dimension reduction of existing attention models may overlook some deep visual semantics, which leads to performance degradation. In this work, we propose an Efficient Multi-Scale Attention (EMA) module, which not only improves the representation capacity but also maintains the efficiency. Unlike previous attention models that reduce the channel dimension, the proposed EMA module maintains the original channel number by reshaping the half channels into the batch dimension and grouping the rest channels into multiple sub-group features with evenly distributed spatial semantic information. To capture the multi-scale dependencies of the channel features, the EMA module adopts large-scale parallel subnetworks to obtain the global channel calibration features. The outputs of the parallel subnetworks are then merged by cross-spatial learning that utilizes the cross-dimension interaction to model the essential pixel-level pairwise cross-dimension relationships among the group features. Experimental results show that the proposed EMA module achieves better performance than the state-of-the-art attention models on CIFAR-100, ImageNet-1k, MS COCO and VisDrone2019 datasets while with fewer parameters and lower computational cost in image classification and object detection tasks.
